episode.title: E173: Google buying HubSpot? FTX depositors not made whole, AI job fears, Ukraine joining NATO
episode.description: Sample structured episode data for the All-In Podcast Search utility.
episode.summary: A sample episode covering FTX restitution, Trump Media, a potential HubSpot acquisition, AI job anxiety, and NATO escalation risk.

chunks.speaker: Jason
chunks.text: The group opens with housekeeping, a quick note on the show's operating cadence, and a reset on why tighter preparation matters when the news cycle is moving this fast.

chunks.speaker: Chamath
chunks.text: They revisit the FTX restitution story and focus on the distinction between nominal recovery and the opportunity cost depositors absorbed while their capital was locked up.
chunks.excerpt: They revisit the FTX restitution story and focus on the distinction between nominal recovery and the opportunity cost depositors absorbed...

chunks.speaker: David Friedberg
chunks.text: Trump Media becomes the next example of how narrative, float, and momentum can overwhelm a sober view of underlying economics for a stretch of time.

chunks.speaker: David Sacks
chunks.text: The HubSpot discussion turns into a broader antitrust and platform strategy debate, with the group weighing whether a major software buyer would create leverage or simply invite regulators to intervene.

chunks.speaker: Chamath
chunks.text: AI job loss fears come up through the lens of real workflow changes, not science fiction, with emphasis on which white-collar tasks are easiest to compress first.

chunks.speaker: Jason
chunks.text: The NATO segment focuses on escalation risk, U.S. election politics, and whether public signaling can drift into commitments that are harder to unwind later.
